hypothesis: An equivariant graph neural network trained on the XDIP dataset to predict Fe K-edge XANES spectra from local atomic structure will achieve significantly lower prediction error (MAE reduction >20%) compared to standard GIN/GAT baselines when the input graph representation explicitly encodes second-shell coordination geometry (bond angles, dihedral angles, and second-neighbor distances beyond the first coordination sphere), because the pre-edge and edge features of Fe K-edge XANES are known to be sensitive to both the symmetry and the extended geometric environment of the iron center, information that is systematically absent from the first-shell-only graph representations used in the current baseline models.
